CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100041890) FILED BY PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONALBANK SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK, FSB ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 4.0 HOURS

DISCOVERY 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS NOTICE OF SUBMISSION ON PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S MOTION ACCORDINGLY THE COURT WILL ISSUE THE FOLLOWING ORDER: DEFENDANTS IS ORDERED TO SERVE PLAINTIFF WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO ALL REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO DO SO, ALL REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WILL BE PRECLUDED TO INTRODUCE ANY EVIDENCE CHALLENGING THE ADMISSION. MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $360.00, PAYMENT OF WHICH SHOULD BE MADE WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON MARCH 20, 2019. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2021 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-16-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Notice Of Submission On Plaintiffs Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK'S Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Notice Of Submission On Plaintiffs Motion To Deem Matters Admitted.

No opposition has been filed against plaintiff's motion to deem matters admitted and for imposition of sanctions, accordingly the court will issue the following order: defendants is ordered to serve plaintiff within ten (10) days from the date of this order code compliant responses without objection to all requests for admission. If defendant fails to do so, all requests for admission will be deemed admitted and defendant will be precluded to introduce any evidence challenging the admission. Monetary sanctions against defendant in the amount of $360.00, payment of which should be made within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare an appropriate order, submit a copy to counsel for defendant for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on March 20, 2019.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Set For Trial On Jul-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
